title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited because they lack persistent context and memory across interactions, comparing them to an expert engineer with amnesia who can only see small portions of code. He advocates for fixing this fundamental architectural problem in agentic systems.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx argues that today's coding agents are limited by a structural flaw: they lack persistent context and memory across interactions, leaving them able to see only small portions of a codebase at a time. He frames this as a genius with amnesia, an expert engineer whose capability is undercut by an inability to retain knowledge between tasks. The talk treats this as a fundamental architectural problem in agentic systems rather than a model quality issue, and calls for fixing memory and context as the path to more capable agents.
